title: Personal/Grooming Supplies Donation Programs - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Volunteering & Donating > Personal/Grooming Supplies Donation Programs 50 Matching Service Providers The mapped locations are estimates, for the exact location of an individual site, select "More Details" Refine Results - Select - No Filter Disaster/Emergency Issues Women Accessibility Options - Select - No Filter Wheelchair Access (Full) Wheelchair Access (Partial) Accessible Parking (Private Lot) Accessible Parking (Nearby Public/Street Parking) Accessible Restrooms Alternative Communication Formats Virtual Service Options Staff Trained in Disability Awareness GLIDE REVITALIZATION DONATIONS PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Accepts small donations of shelf-stable food, pet food, clothing, small appliances, compact furniture, and household goods. Cannot accept large appliances or big furniture. Supports individuals and families impacted by the March 2025 floods in Douglas County. 301 Glide Loop Drive Glide, OR 97443 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Thursday 8am-4pm Email (541) 671-0189 Get Directions Visit Website More Details ASTORIA RESCUE MISSION COMMUNITY SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Serves three community meals including breakfast, lunch, and dinner. Operates a clothing closet, showers, and a laundry facility allowing one load per week. Accepts donations of hygiene products such as shampoo, conditioner, body wash, toilet paper, and toothpaste. 62 W Bond Street Astoria, OR 97103 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Breakfast: 7 days per week 7am Lunch: 7 days per week noon Dinner: 7 days per week 5:30pm Showers: 7 days per week 3:30pm-5pm Laundry: 7 days per week 3:30pm-5pm Clothing Closet: 7 days per week 11am-5pm Email (503) 325-6243 Get Directions Visit Website More Details BEAVERCREEK UNITED CHURCH OF CHRIST BEAVERCREEK FOOD PANTRY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides emergency food boxes and personal hygiene items. Accepts donations of cash, food and hygiene items (soap, shampoo, feminine hygiene products, and toilet paper). 23345 S Beavercreek Road Beavercreek, OR 97004 Hours: 1st and 3rd Thursday of the month 10am-11am Service may extend to weekends and after hours when needed, bring boxes or bags for food. Email (971) 359-3707 Get Directions Visit Website More Details BOARDMAN FOOD PANTRY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a food pantry and toiletry items to those in need. Food, toiletry, and financial donations accepted. Volunteers welcomed. 314 Locust Road SW Boardman, OR 97818 Eligibility: Unrestricted. One food box per household per week. Hours: Monday 9:30am-11am, Thursday 6pm-7pm Email (541) 219-9535 Get Directions Visit Website More Details BROOKINGS CORE RESPONSE PEER HOUSE NAVIGATION CENTER PEER HOUSE DONATIONS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Accepts donations of clothing, personal grooming supplies, and pet food. Does not accept open or used personal grooming supplies. 16389 Highway 101 S Brookings, OR 97415 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Thursday 9am-3pm Email (541) 251-0825 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CASCADE AIDS PROJECT ESTHER'S PANTRY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a shopping-style food pantry, pet food, and personal care items that are available once a month. People without an HIV diagnosis may be eligible to receive a one-time food box of pantry staple items. Accepts donations of shelf-stable food, pet food, toilet paper, and personal care items. 10202 SE 32nd Avenue Suite 601 Milwaukie, OR 97222 Eligibility: Individuals diagnosed with HIV who have a monthly income below 185% of the Federal Poverty Level Hours: Monday 1pm-4pm, Tuesday/Thursday 2pm-5pm, Wednesday 1pm-3pm Email (503) 349-4699 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CHANCE 2ND CHANCE SHELTER EMERGENCY SHELTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides shelter for a maximum capacity of 20 individuals. Laundry, mail, hygiene items, a clothing closet, transitional housing, and other supportive services are available to guests. Accepts donations of hygiene items and new or used (must be laundered in advance) clothing. 1100 Jackson Street SE Albany, OR 97321 Eligibility: Age 18 and older. Persons with sex offender or arson convictions are ineligible. Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-4pm (541) 967-8545 Get Directions Visit Website More Details DRESS FOR SUCCESS OREGON DONATION CALL OUT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Accepts donations of business casual, casual, and trade clothing, scrubs, coats, shoes, purses, backpacks, new undergarments, makeup, toiletries, and jewelry. 1532 NE 37th Avenue Portland, OR 97232 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Donations are accepted on monthly donation days Email (503) 249-7300 107 Get Directions Visit Website More Details FOOD FOR FAMILIES DONATIONS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Accepts donations of shelf stable food items such as oils, flour, canned proteins, spices, and shelf stable milk. Also accepts donations of diapers, dog and cat food, personal and feminine hygiene products, canvas grocery bags, quart and gallon resealable plastic bags, disposable gloves, and produce bags. 3505 SE 182nd Avenue Gresham, OR 97030 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: No standard hours Email (971) 220-6679 Get Directions Visit Website More Details HELPING HANDS REENTRY OUTREACH CENTERS HELPING HANDS REENTRY BYBEE LAKES HOPE CENTER DONATION PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Accepts donations of personal care items such as shampoo, soap, toothpaste, toilet paper, deodorant, and feminine hygiene products. Also accepts financial donations. 14355 N Bybee Lake Court Portland, OR 97203 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: No standard hours Email Get Directions Visit Website More Details HELPING HANDS REENTRY OUTREACH CENTERS HELPING HANDS REENTRY CLATSOP COUNTY DONATION PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Accepts donations of personal care items such as shampoo, soap, toothpaste, toilet paper, deodorant, and feminine hygiene products. Also accepts financial donations. 286 W Marine Drive Astoria, OR 97103 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: No standard hours Email Get Directions Visit Website More Details HELPING HANDS REENTRY OUTREACH CENTERS HELPING HANDS REENTRY SEASIDE DONATION PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Accepts donations of personal care items such as shampoo, soap, toothpaste, toilet paper, deodorant, and feminine hygiene products. Also accepts financial donations. 1530 S Roosevelt Drive Seaside, OR 97138 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: No standard hours Email Get Directions Visit Website More Details HELPING HANDS REENTRY OUTREACH CENTERS HELPING HANDS REENTRY TILLAMOOK COUNTY DONATION PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Accepts donations of personal care items such as shampoo, soap, toothpaste, toilet paper, deodorant, and feminine hygiene products. Also accepts financial donations. 6505 Headquarter Street Tillamook, OR 97141 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: No standard hours Email Get Directions Visit Website More Details HELPING HANDS REENTRY OUTREACH CENTERS HELPING HANDS REENTRY YAMHILL COUNTY DONATION PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Accepts donations of personal care items such as shampoo, soap, toothpaste, toilet paper, deodorant, and feminine hygiene products. Also accepts financial donations. 28380 NE Wilsonville Road Newberg, OR 97132 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: No standard hours Email Get Directions Visit Website More Details HOPE ON THE HILL FOOD PANTRY AND CLOTHING CLOSET Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF A shopping-style pantry and clothing closet (items may include adult and children's clothing and personal care items). One food box or clothing closet visit a month per family. 500 NW Hill Road McMinnville, OR 97128 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Food Pantry: Tuesday 11am-1pm, Thursday 6pm-8pm Clothing Closet: Saturday 10am-noon Donations: Tuesday 11am-1pm, Thursday 6pm-8pm or by appointment Email (503) 472-8476 Get Directions Visit Website More Details IMPACT NW IMPACT NW DANCING TREE RESOURCE CENTER WORKER OF THE DAY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides help navigating resources in the community, information about coordinated access, and information and referral services. Assists on a first-come, first-serve basis with resource information and referral. Supplies fluctuate, but when available may provide Trimet day passes, hygiene items, water/snacks, and clothing. Referrals given for services or items of need that are not accessible through the agency. Donations of unisex clothing and household item are accepted. 10055 E Burnside Street Portland, OR 97216 Eligibility: Varies by service Hours: Office: Monday-Thursday 9am-noon and 1pm-4pm Donations: Monday-Thursday 9am-4pm Email (503) 721-1740 Get Directions Visit Website More Details JOIN JOIN DONATIONS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Accepts donations of food, blankets, sleeping bags, tarps, camping equipment, socks, underwear, hats, gloves, jackets, and hygiene items (shampoo, conditioner, razors, deodorant, feminine products, etc.) 1435 NE 81st Avenue Suite 100 Portland, OR 97213 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday/Wednesday/Friday/Saturday/Sunday 9am-5pm, Tuesday/Thursday 10am-4pm. Closed on national holidays. Email (971) 710-5768 Get Directions Visit Website More Details JUST COMPASSION RESOURCE CENTER DONATIONS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a donation point to drop off items such as clothing, hygiene products, pet supplies, and more. Also accepts financial donations. 12264 SW Hall Boulevard Tigard, OR 97223 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: -Small item donations (such as hygiene products and pet supplies): Drop off Monday 11am-1pm or by appointment -Large item donations (such as furniture, kitchen appliances, and large clothing donations): Drop off by appointment only Email (503) 624-4666 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LIVING WATER FELLOWSHIP LITTLE FREE PANTRY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides an outdoor mini food pantry with shelf-stable food items and personal care items; specific items vary and are not guaranteed. 1000 N Main Avenue Suite 12 Tillamook, OR 97141 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: No standard hours (503) 842-6455 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LOVE INC NEWBERG GAP MINISTRIES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Connects people with local churches and service agencies. Basic goods and services are offered to clients as they are donated. Services available may include, but are not limited to: bicycles, breakfast/lunch bags, clothing closets (adults and children), community meals, counseling services, diapers, haircuts, H.U.G.S. (hats, umbrellas, gloves, scarves, and socks), kitchen supplies, laundry service, bedding/linens, personal grooming supplies/toiletries, over-the-counter medications, personal care kits, physical therapy, showers, water bill assistance, and firewood. Accepts donations of bedding and linen, clothing, and personal grooming supplies. 200 S College Street Newberg, OR 97132 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Thursday 10am-1pm Email (503) 537-3999 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LOVE INC OF CLACKAMAS COUNTY GAP RESOURCES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides furniture, kitchen items, firewood, medical equipment, personal hygiene supplies, cleaning supplies, and bedding/linens. Accepts donations of similar items. Assistance depends on availability. Follow-ups may be provided once the need is met. Eligibility: Low-income households (self-declared or referred and verified by caseworker, employer, or landlord) Hours: Tuesday/Thursday 10am-1pm Email (503) 650-0153 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LOVE INC OF GREATER HILLSBORO GAP MINISTRIES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides essential household items, including beds and bedding, linens, furniture, clothing, diapers, hygiene products, cleaning and laundry supplies, paper goods, and work boots. Donations are accepted of the items the agency distributes, with an ongoing need for couches, kitchen tables, and queen-size or smaller beds (including mattresses, box springs, and frames). Referrals to local food pantries are available. Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday noon-2:30pm, Tuesday/Thursday 9:30am-noon Email (503) 917-0205 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LOVE INC OF TIGARD, TUALATIN, AND SHERWOOD GAP MINISTRIES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides clothing, hygiene supplies, household goods, bedding/linens, over-the counter medicines and supplies, financial training, assistance with resume writing, and nutrition courses. Requests are screened and referred to outside agencies for fulfillment. Assistance is dependent upon available funding and not guaranteed. Also accepts donations of bedding/linens and personal grooming supplies. 10900 SW 121st Avenue Tigard, OR 97223 Eligibility: Varies by service Hours: Monday-Wednesday 9:30am-12:30pm Email (503) 443-6888 Get Directions Visit Website More Details MARY'S RIVER GLEANERS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Members glean food from fields and orchards and collect both food and non-food items donated by stores and markets. The food is distributed weekly through the participating efforts of its members. Incontinence supplies are available. Firewood for heating fuel is often available. Accepting donations of hygiene products such as toothbrushes, toothpaste, shampoo, conditioner, soap, and body wash. 1300 Philomath Boulevard Corvallis, OR 97333 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Distribution: Saturday 11:30am-2pm. Gleaning hours vary. (541) 497-9019 Get Directions Visit Website More Details MY FATHER'S HOUSE MY FATHER'S HOUSE DONATION CENTER DONATION CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Accepts donations of food, cleaning supplies, paper products, diapers, office supplies, wrapping paper, bus tickets, grocery gift cards, and new toys. 5003 W Powell Boulevard Gresham, OR 97030 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Saturday 9am-1pm (503) 492-3046 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OUR JUST FUTURE DONATIONS AND VOLUNTEERING Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Accepts donations of food, personal hygiene products, household cleaning products, diapers, formula, baby powder, wipes, underwear, bras, socks, sleepwear, and cold weather gear. Also offers volunteer opportunities. 10550 NE Halsey Street Portland, OR 97220 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Varies Email (971) 610-1827 Get Directions Visit Website More Details PDX DIAPER BANK PDX DIAPER BANK DONATIONS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Accepts donations of children's disposable diapers (opened packages are accepted), baby wipes, children's cloth diapers and covers (gently used are accepted), and adult incontinence care supplies. Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: No standard hours Email Get Directions Visit Website More Details PORTLAND RESCUE MISSION PORTLAND RESCUE MISSION BURNSIDE SHELTER DONATION PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Accepts donations of blankets, linens in new or nearly new condition (twin sheets, comforters, pillowcases), men's, women's, and children's clothing, travel-sized body wash, soap, shampoo, conditioner, deodorant, toothbrushes, toothpaste, razors, and other personal hygiene items. Food items also accepted. No car seats, furniture, or appliances (large or small). 111 W Burnside Street Portland, OR 97209 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days a week Email (503) 647-7466 Get Directions Visit Website More Details PORTLAND RESCUE MISSION PORTLAND RESCUE MISSION SHEPHERD'S DOOR DONATION PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Accepts donations of blankets, linens in new or nearly new condition (twin sheets, comforters, pillowcases), men's, women's, and children's clothing, travel-sized body wash, soap, shampoo, conditioner, deodorant, toothbrushes, toothpaste, razors, and other personal hygiene items. Food items also accepted. No car seats, furniture, or appliances (large or small). 13207 NE Halsey Street Portland, OR 97230 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday/Wednesday/Thursday 1:15pm-3:45pm Email (503) 647-7466 Get Directions Visit Website More Details RAPHAEL HOUSE OF PORTLAND RAPHAEL HOUSE OF PORTLAND DONATION DROP OFF DONATION PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Accepts donations of new, unopened, and full-size personal hygiene products such as shampoo, conditioner, and body wash. Also accepts toothpaste and toothbrushes, baby hygiene products, natural household cleaning products, high-efficiency laundry detergent, new pillows, new socks and undergarments (for adults and children), twin sheets and blankets, and kitchenware. Does not accept travel-size toiletries, used clothing or shoes, used toys, furniture, cell phones, or any item not in good, clean, working condition. 6161 SE Stark Street Portland, OR 97215 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: 1st Thursday of the month 11am-1pm, 3rd Thursday of the month 4pm-6pm Email (503) 243-5159 - Get Directions Visit Website More Details RIGHT 2 DREAM TOO DONATIONS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Accepts donations of camping gear (tents and sleeping bags), clothing (socks, underwear, shoes, men’s and women’s apparel), bedding (pillows and blankets), toiletries (soap, shampoo, conditioner, toothbrushes, toothpaste, deodorant, body wipes), food (cooked or uncooked), and operational supplies (55-gallon trash bags, paper plates, plastic cutlery). Cash or check donations can be made onsite to support utility and material needs; receipts are available at the front desk. Financial donations may also be mailed to the registered office or made online. Also advocates for policies addressing homelessness and offers volunteer opportunities. 999 N Thunderbird Way Portland, OR 97227 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Saturday 8am-10pm Email Get Directions Visit Website More Details ROSEBURG DREAM CENTER PERSONAL GROOMING SUPPLIES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides hygiene and feminine products, children’s diapers, and incontinence supplies. Accepts donations 2555 NE Diamond Lake Boulevard Roseburg, OR 97470 Eligibility: Hygiene kits: People experiencing homelessness Diapers: Children Incontinence Supplies: Adults Hours: Monday 10am-2pm and 4pm-7pm, Thursday 10am-2pm Email (541) 673-5918 Get Directions Visit Website More Details SAFE OF COLUMBIA COUNTY THE SAFE HOUSE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides temporary shelter from domestic and/or sexual violence, stalking, and sex trafficking. The shelter is confidential, dormitory style housing. Provides personal care items such as soap, shampoo and conditioner, deodorant, toothbrush, and toothpaste when available. Accepts donations of gently used clothing for women and children, and hygiene items. 2194 Columbia Boulevard Saint Helens, OR 97051 Eligibility: Individuals who identify as female and their children (ages 0-18) who are survivors of domestic and sexual violence, stalking, and sex trafficking who currently need to flee a dangerous situation and have no safe place to stay for the night. Male-identified individuals will be referred to available men's shelters. Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (503) 397-7710 Get Directions Visit Website More Details SAINT ANDRE BESSETTE CATHOLIC CHURCH DONATION PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Accepts donations of non-perishable food, adult clothing, winter coats, rain coats, and personal hygiene items by appointment only. Capacity to accept donations may be limited. 601 W Burnside Street Portland, OR 97209 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Tuesday-Wednesday 9:30am-10:30am, Thursday 9:30am-10:30am and 1pm-4pm, Friday 6:30pm-7:30pm Email (503) 228-0746 Get Directions Visit Website More Details SEVENTH DAY ADVENTIST KLAMATH FALLS COMMUNITY SERVICES OUTREACH Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a community meal and distributes personal hygiene items and household cleaning products. Personal hygiene items include toothpaste, toothbrushes, shampoo, deodorant, razors, toilet paper, sanitary napkins, and baby wipes. Cleaning products include laundry soap, dish soap, and household cleaners. Also accepts donations. 1735 Main Street Klamath Falls, OR 97601 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday 5:30pm-6:30pm Email (541) 882-2466 Get Directions Visit Website More Details SEVERE WEATHER JACKSON COUNTY ACCESS MEDFORD SEVERE WEATHER SHELTER DONATIONS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Accepting donations of blankets, pillows, and sheets; hygiene items including feminine products and adult diapers and pads; new and gently used winter clothing; and unopened pet food. 332 W 6th Street Medford, OR 97501 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: No standard hours (458) 488-1220 Get Directions Visit Website More Details SEVERE WEATHER JACKSON COUNTY ASHLAND EMERGENCY WINTER SHELTER DONATIONS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Accepts donations of clothing, personal hygiene items, shelf stable food, pet food, bedding and linen, camping gear and tents, and monetary. Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Office: Monday-Friday 9am-2pm Email (541) 631-2235 Get Directions Visit Website More Details SEVERE WEATHER LANE COUNTY FIRST CHRISTIAN CHURCH HELPING HEARTS ROOM DONATIONS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Accepts donations of clothing, light blankets and bedding items, sleeping bags, tarps, tents, and personal hygiene items. Volunteer opportunities are also available. 1166 Oak Street Eugene, OR 97401 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Tuesday/Thursday 10am-2pm (541) 344-1425 Get Directions Visit Website More Details SEVERE WEATHER LINCOLN COUNTY LINCOLN CITY COMMUNITY SHELTER AND RESOURCE CENTER DONATIONS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Accepts donations of socks, gloves, jackets, blankets, waterproof shoes, and toilet paper. 2125 NW Highway 101 Lincoln City, OR 97367 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: No standard hours (541) 270-1005 Get Directions Visit Website More Details SEVERE WEATHER MULTNOMAH COUNTY MULTNOMAH COUNTY HELP FOR WHEN IT'S COLD WEBSITE DONATIONS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides information about where to donate winter gear. Items needed vary by shelter. Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Not applicable Get Directions Visit Website More Details SEVERE WEATHER UNION COUNTY RIGHT TRACK RESOURCE CENTER DONATIONS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Accepts donations of socks, gloves, hand warmers, shampoo, and conditioner. There is a donation bin in front of the facility. 501 3rd Street La Grande, OR 97850 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: 7 days per week Saturday, November 15, 2025-Sunday, March 15, 2026 (541) 910-3056 Get Directions Visit Website More Details SNOWCAP COMMUNITY CHARITIES SNOWCAP COMMUNITY CHARITIES DONATIONS DONATIONS AND VOLUNTEERING Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Accepts donations of vehicles, commercially packaged food (canned, boxed, fresh, frozen, or dried), baby food/formula, pet food, clothing, socks, shoes, blankets, bedding, pillows, towels, washcloths, personal care/hygiene products, new toys, books, school supplies, and diapers (infant, toddler, or adult). Offers volunteer opportunities for a variety of roles. 17788 SE Pine Street Portland, OR 97233 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-3pm Email (503) 674-8785 Get Directions Visit Website More Details SOUTH BENTON RESOURCE CENTER FOOD PANTRIES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a food pantry with canned goods, fresh produce, bread, and dairy products. Distributes pet food, diapers, personal care and hygiene products when available. Offers information and referrals to agencies for various assistance. Accepts donations of shelf-stable food, condiments, personal care and hygiene products, diapers, and pet food. 648 Orchard Street Monroe, OR 97456 Eligibility: USDA income guidelines of 300% Federal Poverty Level (FPL) Hours: Tuesday 10am-noon 3rd Thursday of the month 4pm-6pm 1st, 2nd, 4th, and 5th Thursdays of the month 10am-noon Emergency: Call or email if outside of normal pantry hours Email (541) 745-8759 Get Directions Visit Website More Details SOUTH COUNTY COMMUNITY FOOD BANK COMMUNITY FOOD BANK Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides emergency food boxes with a three to five day supply of food. Offers hygiene items, incontinence diapers, and laundry soap. May have infant diapers and formula depending on supplies. Accepts donations. 2041 N Roosevelt Drive Seaside, OR 97138 Eligibility: Must meet USDA income guidelines Hours: Tuesday/Thursday 12:45pm-3:45pm Email (503) 738-9800 Get Directions Visit Website More Details SWEET POTATO'S CLOSET Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides essential resources and support to individuals and families, with a special focus on resource families and at-risk youth. Provides clothing, diapers, wipes, toiletries, hygiene items, and other resources. 167 E Main Street Hermiston, OR 97838 Eligibility: Resource families may access resources as needed. Other clients must be engaged with and accompanied by a community partner. Hours: Monday-Thursday 10am-3pm, Friday 1pm-7pm Email (541) 371-2224 Get Directions Visit Website More Details TERESA MCCORMICK CENTER CLOTHING CLOSET Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides clothing, shoes, bedding, personal hygiene products, and cleaning supplies. Offers a bicycle program for individuals with no other means of transportation when bikes are in stock. Also accepts donations. 2600 S Pacific Highway Medford, OR 97501 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday 2pm-4:30pm, Thursday 10am-1pm Email (541) 864-5098 Get Directions Visit Website More Details TILLAMOOK BAY COMMUNITY COLLEGE TBCC FOOD PANTRY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides canned and fresh foods, culturally familiar foods, pet food, and personal hygiene items, such as toilet paper, toothbrushes, and menstrual care products. All items are subject to availability. Donations of dry or canned goods, baby food or formula, animal food, clothing, diapers, and hygiene products are accepted. 4301 Third Street Tillamook, OR 97141 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Food Pantry: Monday/Wednesday 2pm-6pm, Tuesday 2pm-5pm Donations: Monday-Friday 8:30am-4:30pm (503) 842-8222 Get Directions Visit Website More Details TRANSITION PROJECTS ADMINISTRATIVE OFFICE DONATION PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Accepts year-round donations of non-perishable food, unused personal hygiene products, new or gently used linens, blankets, towels, pillows, adult winter clothing, coats, shoes, underwear, socks and books. Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Tuesday/Friday 9am-noon, Wednesday 3pm-6pm Email (503) 488-7745 Get Directions Visit Website More Details UNITY SHELTER CHAPMAN PLACE HYGIENE CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides showers, laundry facility, clothing, personal care supplies, hot meals four days a week, a to-go meal five days a week, assistance with applying for the Oregon Health Plan (OHP), and information to other health care assistance and social service connections. Accepts donations of paper products, towels, snacks, bottled water, juice, personal care supplies, socks, underwear, and seasonally appropriate clothing. 211 SE Chapman Place Corvallis, OR 97333 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 11am-4pm Meals: Monday/Thursday 5:30pm-6:30pm, Wednesday/Friday 1pm-2pm Donations: Monday-Friday11am-4pm or Corvallis Daytime Drop-in Center Monday-Friday 9am-noon (530 SW 4th Street, Corvallis 97333) Email (541) 286-5121 Get Directions Visit Website More Details VINA MOSES CENTER VINA'S FREE THRIFT STORE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides clothing and shoes including interview-ready clothes, food, home goods, kitchenware (pots, pans, dish sets), and hygiene items. Workforce closet available with work boots and shoes, rain and safety gear. Shopping allowed once per month. Exceptions made for special circumstances. Accepts donations of food, clothing, bedding, linens, kitchenware, toys and games, and hygiene supplies. 968 NW Garfield Avenue Corvallis, OR 97330 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: -Thrift Store: Monday/Wednesday 1pm-3pm, Tuesday/Thursday 10am-noon and 1pm-3pm, 1st Saturday of the month noon-3pm -Donations: Visit website for current hours Email (541) 753-1420 Get Directions Visit Website More
